# Industry Intel — April 28, 2026

*Generated by Paw 🐾 | Sources: Tavily search × 10 + targeted web fetches*

---

## 🚨 1. Flashframe Is Actively Reframing the Take Rate Debate

**What:** Flashframe's March 11 blog post "Economics and sponsorship that don't punish photographers" directly attacks the industry habit of comparing platforms by take rate alone — arguing that photographer revenue share, sponsor subsidies, and gallery economics matter more than FMS-style commission percentages. Their April 13 post "Race Photography Distribution is a workflow, not an ecosystem" further argues that platforms shouldn't require exclusive timing-company lock-in — a shot at integrated models like what FMS is building with RunRio.

**Why it matters to FMS:** Flashframe is writing content that directly pre-empts FMS's competitive pitch. If Lenli or Van are pitching new organizers in the US or SEA, prospects may have already read Flashframe's take. Their 45% take rate advantage post-Rio becomes harder to defend if organizers frame the question as "but what do photographers actually earn?"

**👉 Action/Watch:** Lenli should read both Flashframe posts and prepare a counter-narrative one-pager for US/SEA organizer pitches — lead with photographer earnings *in absolute PHP/USD*, not just take rate percentages.
🔗 https://www.flashframe.io/blog/

---

## 🚨 2. Flashframe Added Face Recognition in February 2026

**What:** Flashframe launched facial recognition search as a complement to bib tagging in late February 2026 — allowing runners to find photos even when bibs are obscured. It's positioned as a secondary discovery path, not a replacement for bib search.

**Why it matters to FMS:** FMS's face recognition (AWS Rekognition) has been live longer, but Flashframe is now feature-parity on this front with Western organizers. The window to claim "only platform with face search" is closed in the US market. FMS's edge must shift to depth (bib + face + prepay integration) and SEA-market fit.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Tim should document FMS's face + bib + prepay stack as a bundled moat in the product one-pager — not just face recognition in isolation. This is the pitch for Chicago meetings.
🔗 https://www.flashframe.io/blog/face-recognition-is-here-a-faster-way-to-find-and-sell-race-photos-on-flashframe/

---

## 🚨 3. 9pic.ai Launches BibTrack — New AI Race Photo Entrant

**What:** 9pic.ai has launched "BibTrack," an AI-powered photo tagging product for race events featuring BIB + name mapping, partial BIB matching, and what appears to be a SaaS tool for organizers to self-serve. Positioning is as a white-label/API tool rather than a full marketplace.

**Why it matters to FMS:** This is a new entrant targeting the same workflow pain point (tagging speed). If 9pic.ai prices itself as a cheap self-serve tool, it could undercut FMS on small-to-mid tier events — exactly the segment FMS needs to fill outside Rio. Worth tracking pricing and organizer targets.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Van or BizDev should create a 9pic.ai entry in the competitor dossiers folder. Check if they've appeared in any PH or SEA running communities yet.
🔗 https://9pic.ai/products/bibtrack/

## 🌏 4. KL Standard Chartered Marathon 2026 Registration Opening Soon

**What:** The Kuala Lumpur Standard Chartered Marathon 2026 (KLSCM) has opened an Active Lifestyle Expo partnership page and registration is imminent. KLSCM is one of SEA's largest marathons, attracting 20,000+ runners and significant international participation. They're actively seeking exhibitors and partners.

**Why it matters to FMS:** KLSCM is exactly the caliber of SEA event FMS should be targeting for a pilot outside PH. It's a prestige race, photo commerce is underdeveloped in Malaysia, and the expo partnership angle gives FMS an entry point that's lower-commitment than full exclusivity. This is Q3 2026 SEA expansion territory.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Lenli should reach out to KLSCM organizers this week as an expo partner or photo platform pilot — frame it as "no cost to the organizer, we bring the photographer network, you get a professional photo experience." Lock in before Flashframe or Sportograf get there.
🔗 https://www.kl-marathon.com/

## 🌏 5. Singapore International Marathon 2026 Is SEA's Only World Athletics Gold Label Race

**What:** The 2026 BYD Singapore International Marathon (SIM) is confirmed and marketing actively — Southeast Asia's only World Athletics Gold Label road race. It's sponsored by BYD and presented by adidas, signaling major brand investment in the event.

**Why it matters to FMS:** Gold Label races attract elite runners and significant international media. A photo partnership here would be a marquee reference customer for FMS's SEA credibility — especially when pitching future organizers in Thailand and Indonesia. The brand sponsor interest also signals photo monetization potential is high.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Add Singapore International Marathon 2026 to the SEA organizer outreach list. This is a stretch goal — approach after KLSCM pilot is secured, but make contact now so FMS is in the conversation.
🔗 https://singaporeinternationalmarathon.com/

## 💡 6. Evoto Instant AI — Real-Time Photo Delivery Becoming Table Stakes

**What:** Evoto Instant (by TrueSight) now offers a full AI-powered real-time event photo workflow: auto-culling (removing closed eyes, blurry/over/under-exposed shots), live upload, instant gallery, and real-time sharing — all managed from camera to participant within minutes of capture. Multiple platforms (Kamero.ai, Evoto) are now shipping this capability.

**Why it matters to FMS:** "Photos available within hours" is no longer a differentiator — it's becoming baseline. If FMS's photographer partners are still on overnight upload workflows, FMS risks looking slow vs. competitors. Real-time delivery also increases impulse purchase conversion, which directly supports FMS's conversion rate improvement goals.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Tim should evaluate integrating real-time upload/delivery capability for FMS photographer partners as a Q3 product priority. Even a basic "live gallery" feature could meaningfully lift conversion rates.
🔗 https://instant.evoto.ai/

## 💰 7. Foxmont Capital Plans PHP 4 Billion PH Startup Investment

**What:** Foxmont Capital Partners announced a plan to invest up to PHP 4 billion in the Philippine startup ecosystem, welcomed by the Department of Finance's Secretary Frederick Go. This signals continued investor confidence in PH tech and will likely spur more early-stage deal flow.

**Why it matters to FMS:** As FMS approaches incorporation and fundraising, a more active PH VC market is good news. Foxmont has invested in Philippine consumer tech and marketplace businesses before — FMS's photo commerce + AI angle could be compelling. Also validates that PH is a credible startup domicile for SEA expansion stories.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Van should have Dave flag Foxmont as a potential strategic investor to approach post-incorporation. Don't pitch now — wait until Rio signing is confirmed and Phase 1 numbers are visible.
🔗 https://www.dof.gov.ph/sec-frederick-go-welcomes-foxmonts-php4-billion-investment-plan-to-grow-ph-startup-ecosystem/

## 🌏 8. SEA Payment Methods 2026: QR Dominance Across Every Market

**What:** The Worldpay Global Payments Report 2026 confirms that QR code-based payments now dominate across all major SEA markets — GCash/Maya in PH, GoPay/QRIS in Indonesia, PromptPay in Thailand, DuitNow in Malaysia, PayNow in Singapore. Cash is declining even at street vendors. Each country has its own dominant rail.

**Why it matters to FMS:** FMS's SEA expansion requires market-specific payment integration, not just a single gateway. USD via Stripe works for the US, but Thailand needs PromptPay, Malaysia needs DuitNow. If Tim's payment scoping doesn't include SEA rails, FMS will hit a wall in Q3 when the first non-PH events go live.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Tim should add SEA payment methods (per-country) to the prepay integration scope discussion this week — don't let US-readiness scoping assume SEA uses the same rails.
🔗 https://fintechnews.sg/128337/e-commerce/southeast-asia-payment-methods-2026-global-payments-report/

---

## ⚠️ 9. Facial Recognition Privacy Enforcement Surging in 2026

**What:** TrustArc and Orrick Law both report that privacy regulators in the US and EU are increasingly scrutinizing facial recognition deployments at public events and venues. US state laws (Illinois BIPA, Texas CUBI) require explicit consent before biometric data collection. The LA Clippers' Intuit Dome face authentication system (opt-in selfie upload) is the model regulators seem to prefer — consent-first, not passive scan-and-match.

**Why it matters to FMS:** FMS uses AWS Rekognition for face search. In the PH market, this is currently low-risk — PH's Data Privacy Act is less prescriptive than BIPA. But as FMS enters the US market (Chicago meetings!), any face recognition feature could trigger legal scrutiny. Passive face matching of race participants without explicit consent is legally risky in multiple US states.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Before the Chicago meetings, Van should confirm with legal (or Dave) whether FMS's face recognition feature needs a US-specific consent flow. Worst case: disable face search for US events until consent UX is built. Don't let a $500K legal risk kill a $50K deal.
🔗 https://www.orrick.com/en/Insights/2025/11/Facial-Recognition-Technology-Use-in-Stadiums-Key-Takeaways

---

## 📅 10. Triathlon Participation Surging Globally in 2026 — Ironman, T100, Challenge Family All Growing

**What:** Triathlete Magazine cites expert consensus that 2026 is a breakout year for triathlon participation globally — driven by wearable data culture, social media race content, and post-COVID fitness momentum. Major series (Ironman, T100, XTERRA) all reported 2025 participation growth and expect 2026 to accelerate. An estimated 3.1M Americans alone did at least one tri-related event in 2023, with the number climbing.

**Why it matters to FMS:** Spartan SEA (Rio's other property) is in the obstacle/endurance space. Triathlon growth = more high-value endurance participants who are exactly FMS's buyer profile — motivated, finisher-proud, and willing to pay for race photos. Triathlon events in PH/SEA (like IRONMAN 70.3 Philippines) are an underserved photo opportunity for FMS.

**👉 Action/Watch:** Add triathlon organizers in PH (IRONMAN Philippines, Sunrise Events) to the organizer outreach pipeline alongside running events — these are premium events with participants who have higher photo purchase intent.
🔗 https://www.triathlete.com/culture/news/5-predictions-for-the-triathlon-world-in-2026/
